review_text: Recently stayed on a wimm, we needed somewhere to stay last minute, with our new caravan, they took us with no issues with our dog aswell, wonderful service, nice place, woman's amenities were lovely and clean, big clean  park, would stay again

I stayed a long weekend camping with my best friend. We were booked on site 16 which unfortunately was not suitable for swag set up due to rocks I went to reception and the amazing young lady was so happy to help me get onto a site that would suit us site 23 great site for tents and swags. The shower/toilets are beautiful and clean. Camp kitchen was easy to access and clean. Great location if staying with kids as Australia Zoo is only 500 metres down the road.

Again beautiful quite spot and amazing Staff 😊
Wonderful cabins with a beautiful view!
Nice getaway to visit Australia zoo

Perfect distance if you are heading to Australia Zoo. 4 mins away.
Amenities are kept relatively clean, grounds are kept well. Staff were lovely and super helpful.
Was lovely to see a stall with jams, preserves, sauces on the Friday we were there. Stocked up on them!

We stayed 2 nights and had a great time. Amenities were clean and convenient, kitchen had all a family could need. The powered sites were spacious. The campsite is within a wooded area, felt nice to be in a natural surrounding and the kids loved the ducks and turtles in the pond and the firepit. Would visit again.
excellent dog friendly park , fantastic staff . top relaxing setting . highly recommend ,generous sites easy access
a super friendly reception, it was very quiet and very pleasant, toilets and shower clean, the proximity to Australia Zoo was also interesting for us, I would check in here again at any time
This is a great park. I will definitely come back.
Lovely rooms in a great location, with courtesy shuttle bus to Australia Zoo
I had a pleasant experience living here. It was safe and secure. But there was some lewd behavior from the near by residents. Walking around butt naked and doing indecent things in a public space.
Friendly park with good sized camping sites. Staying longer than anticipated due to very wet weather!
We had a lovely stay and will definitely be back. All staff were very friendly and helpful. All amenities we needed and great location for the zoo

Great little park surrounded by trees, plenty of room for a larger van. Clean ammenities, friendly staff.
Hoping to return sometime

I booked a solo holiday trip in the superior garden view 1 bedroom cabin for 4 nights in early January 2026. For a solo holiday, it was the perfect little cabin to come back to after each day of sightseeing around the area. Great value and will recommend this holiday park to others who journey this way.

Will definitely be booking this place again in the future when I journey up this way.

Hi LHP team, we recently had a week stay for a Sunny Coast cricket carnival. On arrival the groundman mowed into a cricket oval and cricket pitch so the kids could get stuck in. Powered sites are very generous for room and the numerous camp kitchens provided all the required facilities. We called through later in the week about a few extra families for a team BBQ one night. The LHP team encouraged the celebrations and even did an extra clean of the camp kitchen facilities. Had a great stay and hope we end up there again next year.

We arrived after 10hr drive little disappointed. A lot of permanent residents there what good.
But permanent resident were verbal abusing us and any who drove in the entry gates.
The cabin was so little for family of 4. Only 2 seater lounge no chairs for kids sit inside.
The stove top didn’t work. (Tried all week call them due busy scheduled we gone before open and back at close time)
What pictures were shown to what we got was little disappointed 😞.
If no kids would been ok in cabin. Took young kid for walk as he was going stir crazy in such little space and permanent residents weren’t happy he was just running.

If got kids with adhd, autism or anything were they make noise this place not recommended.

If just you and your partner or friend get away it be ok.
The caravan side seems pretty ok from what I seen on my walk kids were able be free.

Pool super small and no play ground area for kids.

The site was pleasant and well equipped, with both power and water available. The kitchen was well stocked, though quite small. The laundry facilities were good, the toilets clean, and the showers spacious. Overall, the campsite is rather small, but functional and comfortable.
Theres nothing there and the pool is tiny and old looks like tge paints all coming off it, not inviting at all and over priced

A brilliant park in a convenient location! Really nice sites, with a lovely outlook over the duck pond, which provides some entertainment for the little ones. The park is located close to everything the coast has to offer. 20 minutes in each direction, and you'll find everything you need. Shops close by, along with plenty of places to eat. The toilets could do with refurbishment and a deep clean. The highlight of the stay was the lovely community garden, run by the local residents. If you're there in the afternoons, the residents open up their community stall, with the most incredible jams, sauces and some fresh produce, all grown/made by the residents..

Stayed here while visiting Queensland
4x4 and rooftop tent.
Very relaxed and quiet atmosphere
Great facilities too

Stayed for 25 nights (in our caravan with 2 cats)  before and over the Christmas period visiting family.  Staff very friendly and accommodating.  The park is lovely, with lots of trees and a lagoon.  We loved feeding the ducks, water birds, eels and water dragons.  The park is close to Australia Zoo, Landsborough shops, and not too far to Beerwah or Caloundra. Will definitely stay again next time when we visit family.
Caravan park ok.  Amenities ok. Nothing to rave about.

The position of the park is beside a major intersection, and while traffic noise itself isn't too bad, the hoons on bikes and in cars destroy the serinity. The park's roads badly need repair, as do the amenities.

Es war eine unglaubliche, einmalige Erfahrung in solch einem wunderschönen Hotel mit Geschichte, toller, einmaliger Kunst, wundervollem Personal, eigener Butler für ein paar Tage in diese Welt einzutauchen.|Herzlichen Dank für die tolle  Erfahrung, wir kommen sehr gerne wieder.
Nice little park stayed there for a couple of nights nice and clean amenities were clean managers where great would stay again would recommend cheers mal

Such a gem of a place, beautiful quiet spot which is not far from all the major tourist spots including  Australia Zoo and Glasshouse Mountains.
Beautiful clean spacious amenities and friendly receptionist. Just disappointed that we could only stay 1 night so make sure you book as it's not a huge park. We will definitely recommend staying here.
The cabin was perfect for what I needed~ clean, well appointed and easily accessible. The park has a good vibe and the location was on point. Will definitely come back again! Thank you 🙏

Sehr Uriger und Wilder Caravan Park. Schön in der Natur gelegen mit kleinem See und Wildtieren. Sanitäranlagen sind OK der Pool ist etwas zu klein für die Anlage. Stellplätze mit und ohne Betonplatten vorhanden. Der Zoo ist auch nur einen Steinwurf entfernt.

Stayed here recently for a couple of nights & the park was beautiful with clean modern cabins a nice view of the pond lots of beautiful birds & water dragons. The permanent residents gardens are beautiful nice quiet park for a couple nights rest while travelling through and visiting Australia zoo which is only 2 minutes away.

Excellent bathroom & toilet facilities SO CLEAN
Great laundry kitchen- sink fridge very clean neat & tidy
Would give this park a 10 if I could

Beautiful, clean and spacious. Had everything we needed for a family stay|The cabin we stayed in was spacious, very clean and came with all of the amenities we could need.|We had a small 2 burner stove top (I wish I knew before!) a microwave and a full size fridge. We were provided pots and pans, sharp knives for cutting, a strainer, mixing bowl, peeler, tongs, cups, glasses, a toaster and kettle, and generously given some tea and coffee which were very appreciated! The staff were extremely kind and understanding of my mix up when booking and were happy to help however they could.|The only negative (and I really had to think hard to find one) was the gravel between the cabins isn't very suitcase friendly. With a family of 5 we had plenty of luggage.|Apart from that super minor inconvenience, the entire stay was beautiful and welcoming.

Amazing Holiday Park, The staff are awesome and look after the place.
Clean bathrooms, close to Australia Zoo and the camp kitchen is really good. The view of the lake is nice with plenty of wildlife to watch

This review is not about the Park it self, it is about following their own the cancelation policy and the service. |Unfortunately we had to cancel hour trip including the stay of 2 nights in Landsborough Holiday Park. after several mails and calls, there was a conformation of the cancelation and the message about the refund.  Now weeks later still no money receipt, and also no reaction anymore.|So if there is a risk that you have to cancel your stay, do not go here. This was the only place  where we did not get the refund and had this bad service.
Omg 😲 how Lovely 😍 👌 👍 🥰 ☺️
Great little park. Spent 4 nights in the camper trailer. Kids loved exploring the creek right next to camp.

We stayed for 3 nights camp tent site with family.
Pros: location close to the nature and amenities. Friendly staff. Small but tidy camp kitchen and toilets. Pet friendly.
Cons: All sites are gravel and road-base no turf. Did not enjoy staying in the tent specially when it felt like a construction site. Needs a drill bit for the pegs.

Go there if you got a caravan or a motor home. Not for tent.
Beautiful and very peaceful park with lots of wildlife. And very convenient if going to Australia Zoo with a shuttle bus pick-up.

Has gotten mouldy in showers not cleaned as much since no longer ingenua
I cleaned laundry emptied bins and swept floor
Filthy in corners

Terrible . The pond /Lake was discusting full of midgies and smell was sickening. Birds & ducks pooping under our awning. Was promised a nice site when booked 4 mths in advance , told  in check in , there were 2 new managers since we booked and site was changed . Plus we booked for 8 mths and left after 3 . Never stay again .
I do work out here but been in homes. Lovely friendly people at reception. Good location.
Lovely community style welcoming caravan park. Some permanent sites, some holiday sites, cabins. Ablutions are clean and hot water is hot. Walking distance to Landsborough town and train station.
We had such a great experience thanks to the friendly and helpful staff! They went above and beyond to make our work function scavenger hunt a successful event. Highly recommend!
We stayed in a 2 bedroom villa.  Was clean, tidy and comfortable.  Location was excellent for visiting Australia Zoo. Very friendly staff.
Lovely spot with pond, caravan sites are nice, drive through sites available

Had a wonderful overnight stay here in one of the cabins. Was travelling through Queensland and had to arrange accommodation with short notice, and the staff at Landsborough Holiday Park were very helpful with sorting it out for me.
Too spot . Great staff
Great quiet little park facilities are lovely and clean well maintained had our caravan here for a few weeks will be back
Best place i have stayed
Beautiful green and excellent location

It was an absolute joke this place!
We had booked an unpowered site for $55 which is completely overpriced for what we got.
They gave us a muddy parking slot right by the pond where there are supposed to be parking slots for 2 cars - absolutely ridiculous! We parked on both of them to have enough space and not fall into the water.
The facilities were okay but have seen better for sure.
The only positive thing was the lady on the phone - she was nice.

But still would never go back there for that price and also not recommend it to anyone if you expect a nice campsite.

Very pretty little park. We had a great site. But it stinks like the pond we were near. I felt really unwell most of our stay as I couldn't get past the turtle and duck dropping smell. Apart from a few rain days, this was a motivated to leave early. Other sites probably would be better, but I could smell it everywhere.
Their laundry was expensive, $7 a load and another $5 to dry. There is a laundromat that is cheaper in town.
You also need to have $1 coins and the cash to buy the coins from the front office, no eftpos for coin.
The toilets and showers were cleanish.
The park itself was very tidy. We never used all the amenities, but the camp kitchen looked well equipped and even had a gas fired pizza oven.
Great stay. Nice and quiet

I would have given 5 stars should the cost have been $45.  $48 a night for a van site is a bit too expensive.  Our stay however was very nice with a little lake that we backed on to complete with ducks and birdlife.  There was a smell at times that wasn't pleasant.  We thought it was bats but we didn't see any.  The staff were friendly.
Lovely setting with the pond in the middle of the park. Facilities overall are clean and tidy.
Great little secluded park a real cabin in the woods
Very good

Very well appointed camp ground with great facilities. Everything was always clean and the camp kitchens were very handy.
Staff were exceedingly friendly, helpful and accommodating.
Relatively close to good beaches and lots to do, see and eat nearby.
We enjoyed our stay and will definitely be back.

We stayed here for 4 days leading up to New Years Eve. We were very happy with the location and found Ingenia to be quiet, well looked after, the cabin we stayed in was as described in the photos and very clean. Arrival was smooth and easy and we would definitely stay here again next time.

1st time staying here & we will definitely be back. Peaceful setting amongst the trees. Our site had a little creek running behind it. Plenty of room, clean facilities. Great water pressure in the shower. Friendly staff. Central location to explore the Sunshine coast beaches or glass house mountains.
So loved staying in the holiday park

Ingenia Holidays Landsborough is a gorgeous place to stay. It has beautiful and tranquil gardens; duck ponds and surrounds that are very well maintained. The manager and all staff are friendly, available and extremely helpful. The location cannot be faulted with this park in the historic town of Landsborough and so close to Australia Zoo and many other attractions.
Small campground with a great camping kitchen and clean facilities. Very quiet but very close to the zoo.
So impressed with the professionalism and customer service.  Really enjoy our stay at the caravan park. Highly recommended
Excellent cabins quiet near the town square good service will be back
One of the best caravan parks we have stayed in. Family friendly, would definitely come back
Staff are very willing to help in seeking  advice, information about local activities and recommendations. Courteous and efficient.

We stayed here to go to Australia Zoo we stayed 3 nights spent 2 days at the zoo, the 2 bedroom cabin was very nice and well kept, clean, comfortable, staff friendly and helpful, lots of wildlife to see in the pond and around, close to zoo. Short walk to shops.

From the receptionist who was one of the friendliest we have encountered in a long time to our perfect drive through site. This is a beautiful tranquil park that has a lovely pond (that is constantly aureate)! Bird life is healthy.
We did not use the facilities, but did inspect. They were lovely and clean and the shower cubical big enough for a party.
Camp kitchens, and a pleasant BBQ area with pizza oven. There is also a communal fire pit. What more can you ask for.
It’s a slice of tranquil paradise that puts you within arms reach of Australian Zoo and the beaches of the Sunshine Coast. Also day visits to the famous Maleny area.
Yep, you guest it! This has now been voted our place to stay when on or near the Sunshine Coast.. also it’s pet friendly!
Keep up the great work to all that are evolved with the running of this park.
Fantastic place to stay, facilities are brilliant, the staff were very nice, it was the young lady's second day and she was very helpful. Had an enjoyable time. Thank you.

This park has huge potential, location & it's ambience are it's strong points, just needs upgrading work on roads, amenities & swimming pool.
Just a short walk into the lovely township.

Lovely, tidy, clean and spacious cabin. Comfortable beds. It was advertised as a Big 4/Ingenia Holiday destination but the majority of the park is permanent residents. Don't feel 100% comfortable in this situation. Feels a bit like I'm staying in someone's back yard. Luckily, we were only there for 2 nights and had somewhere nice to sleep. No catering whatsoever to kids, so if you have active kids and you're not planning a day at Aussie World, you're best to avoid this place. The pool was clean but desperately in need of an upgrade. Also, not all that appreciative of the repeative contact about our ETA, but we did have a seamless check in after hours. Was a pleasant overnighter but useless as a holiday park.
Amenities a bit run down needs an upgrade otherwise ok.
Clean rooms, friendly service, fair pricing and the location is perfect for Australia Zoo
Lovely to camp..friendly they have all you need..close to australian zoo..
Lovely little park in a quiet location only meters from Steve Irwin Way. Great little shopping centre about 500 m from the front gate. Little creek runs asking the back of the caravan sites
Affordable price with every amenities you need in the cabin. Will come back again. Service was brilliant.
Comfortable enough but for me the bunk bed mattress was a little hard.  Staff are great.

Very easy, straightforward and friendly check-in. I was greeted by a young lady who was warm, friendly, helpful and very informative of local activities and services. We checked into the Ducks Hollow 2 bedroom cabin. The cabin was super spacious, modern and comfortable. The grounds were soggy to walk on, but there had been recent downpours of rain. The cabin had a balcony with a table and two chairs which had a lovely view of the lake. The park was very close to Landsborough, about 5 minutes away from Australia Zoo, and 10 minutes to Beerwah. The kitchen was well stocked with all essentials required to cook meals, although a larger frypan would have been helpful. The cabin was large, clean and well looked after. The park was quiet, clean and tidy. It seems that it was predominantly permanent sites, but this didn't bother us at all. A maintenance note was left for the main bedroom door, but maybe some extra space for towel hanging would be helpful. We would definitely stay here again! Thank you for a wonderful stay.

Just spent 10 days in this beautiful caravan park.  Mostly full of permanent vans  but where we stayed had a lovely view of the lagoon. Every morning & evening we were greeted by the birdlife that resides in the park & u can purchase food from reception to feed them. Just up the road from Australia Zoo & within driving distance to the villages in the Hinterland. Would highly recommend this park & will stay here again.

Good park, well run. Only 3 km
from Australia zoo. Easy walk into a nice town

Pretty disappointed in basic communication from this place. Last week I was sent a invoice for 4 years ago. Causing intense trauma not just for the fact most of us don’t have money for surprise bills ( sent in error and blamed on a system update) I explained why this traumatised me being the last family holiday I had with my kids before one was killed in accident. To read only yesterday your place doesn’t take safety seriously is another reason for this feedback. I have been ignored by email simply asking for my details to be removed from their system to prevent things like this causing trauma again. Only email I have received was blaming the system. Take some accountability it goes a long way for business as well as giving customers the simple basic customer service skills by acknowledging their issues not ignoring them. Absolutely appalling

Campsite would be a 3 star rating at best, everything very average, apart from the service which was pretty poor. Paid for a double powered campsite, couldn’t get power. Told them at the office about the issue, was told the handyman would check. Received a text saying the power had been checked, one socket works but don’t use the other (already not ideal when 2 vehicles required power). The one that apparently worked, only worked for around 20 mins before tripping out. Went to see the office again upon leaving just to recommend that the power outlet is looked at properly as it’s potentially dangerous, they didn’t seem bothered nor did they offer any kind of compensation for our 2 nights without power. Pretty poor from a big campsite like this, a simple issue that could have been rectified, but dealt with poorly, would not stay again.

Excellent all round :)
bathrooms
service
location
All great :)
Beautiful park! Clean facilities and super friendly staff. Only 5 mins from Australia Zoo. Our favourite stop so far :)
Nice place to stay while visiting the Australian Zoo.  Plenty of wildlife around the cabins.

We stayed on a unpowered site which was clean, quiet and level (which you don't normally get). The office left us the map and details for the site in a outside box as we were arriving late which were easy to follow. The site was really quiet and the facilities were clean with a great shower. Good value for money!
Nice and clean accommodation

Beautiful camp site right next to a big duck pond and surrounded by the forest. If your going to the zoo they have a 10% discount for all their guest and it's a 5min drive to to zoo, they also have a shuttle bus to the zoo and back.
Totally recommend this place to unwind and the pool after a hole day walking visiting the zoo.
The staff are lovely and are happy to help.
We will be back here!

Unfortunately the permanent residents ruined this one for us. We were over ruled and didn’t feel like we could really enjoy our caravan park because of this. The only paved space for our kids to ride scooters was in the permanent area, and when we did so, the residents told us we weren’t welcomed, to get away. Our kids got scooters from Santa so we’re excited to go away, and to ride them but couldn’t in the rocky ground that was the camp sites. The facilities and the staff were lovely however. And the location is great, near so many activities. But the overall experience for us wasn’t great. Our kids made friends with other campers kids and they were running around in the day time being kids and they kept coming back upset saying “old people are going to the office to make a complaint!”. It was sad for us as all other caravan parks we have visited where our kids make friends and go off and ride their scooters, swim and play etc. We didn’t feel we could enjoy ourselves this time.
Fantastic place to stay and definitely be going back when travelling in this area
Lovely place to stay
Stayed in caravan park. Quiet yet very handy to Zoo, Caloundra and Montville.
The cabins were great plenty of space for 2 adults and 2 kids. Ducks and wildlife around to feed that live in the pond highly recommend
Great stop the night before the zoo
Great place to visit for a quite getaway
Nice spot very helpful girl on the desk went out of her way to help us.shes worth more money

We stayed in one of the cabins and there were a few things that made our stay no so great. There is no WIFI as advertised. The hot water kept cutting off, up to four times in ten minutes so showers were terrible. My allergies flared up badly due to the amount of dust in the cabin and it was confirmed when we checked under the bed and saw a layer of dust underneath. The cabin smelled awful! Particularly the couch and the curtains let off a pungent BO smell. Trains honking through the night so it isn't a peaceful location. And lastly, most of the place is taken up by permanent residents, mainly seniors. It felt like we were holidaying in a retirement village.

It had everything we needed and it was livable but just not up to standard. The price should definitely be lower for what you get i feel ripped off honestly.
Close to Landsborough shopping centre. Powered sites small. Advised staff that a butcher bird was swooping people and drew blood on my husband's head but they didn't seem to care.
We stayed in one of the cabins which was very comfortable and had everything we needed. We had a lovely outlook onto the pond. There was also a back balcony that was lovely and peaceful.

My wife and I stayed here for 4 nights in October. We stayed in the Possums 1 bedroom villa for 2 nights and the Daintree 1 bedroom villa for 2 nights. The accommodation is clean and basic but served our needs and was central to the places we wished to see. The layout was a bit strange with toilet opening straight onto a small kitchen, where as it should be off the bedroom with a sliding door. The photos online make it look much bigger than it is. The kitchen is small with little bench space the oven and stove were functional but pretty old. The bedroom was nice and the bed was comfortable and we had a good nights sleep. The lounge is nice enough and there are outside decks off the bedroom and front. The bathroom had a toilet and shower and was clean and functional. The surroundings are nice and parking is right outside the accommodation. There is a coin operated laundry on site, and a swimming pool that we did not have time to use. We had a pleasant stay and would recommend to travelers who were looking for basic clean accommodation at a reasonable price. The staff were very friendly and helpful, and check in and out was quick and easy.
We had a great unpowered site, which was quiet and we were able to hook up to water for our stay.

First impression park needs money spent on it especially the pool area but once settled in the place became a little slice of nature. Relaxing and everything you need. Watching the 10 or more ducklings being looked after by the older ducks was amuzing. Would come again. Good base to drive to Malany ect.
But yes would like to see pool area improved on.

Petit camping propre et un peu équipé mais proche de la voie ferrée donc un peu bruyant et chère pour les prestations 52$ pour 2 avec un van et l’électricité. Il y une mare avec beaucoup d’oiseaux mais aussi des moustiques. Majorité d’emplacement résidentiel dans ce camping.
Great site near Australia's Zoo, easy walk into town too (good food and pubs in Ealing distance). We arrived late, but everything was ready and prepared for us, thankyou!
Wonderful service from staff, cabins were tidy, well-equipped and  as advertised. 10/10 would recommend.
Lovely place to camp, the cleaniest amenties block, close to nearby attractions. The pool does need renovating though.

Camping sites are fairly good size.
Bathroom has nice shower rooms with a bench.
Kitchen is ok. The oven/stove is currently broken. It has a microwave, kettle, and toaster.
Quiet and clean park. Lots of bird life and an easy walk into town.
Great stay and beautiful caravan park and staff are very helpful

A lovely pet friendly park.

Incredibly impressed with this park, immaculate amenities, family friendly and good use of space.

I will say the rooster next door was a downside but that's not their fault!

Fantastic service, thank you to Shannon who helped with my booking. The cabin was very clean and very comfortable. You had everything you needed great animenties. Beautiful surroundings and was quiet at night. Very close to the shops, bus and train service if needed. All and all it was a last minute booking for me and very glad I chose them.
I love staying there it is very peaceful and quite

I feel the common areas could do with a bit more cleaning. Main kitchen is a tiny bit too small for the amount of camper spaces.

Otherwise the location is absolutely beautiful, the little pond is just wow. Nature is on fire.

Reception staff so friendly, sweet and welcoming.

We camped so we cannot comment on the cabins :)

I am so sorry to say that our time was cut short. WE left, with no sleep for the 2 nights we did stay, due to trains honking all night and lyre birds calling/singing. I am even sadder to say that the facilities were not clean, No hand soap was filled for our entire stay, I waited for the floors to be cleaned, then arrived just after and found the mop filthy, the floors had been 'swished' with a dirty  mop, the dirt rubbed into the tile grooves and visible throughout the facilities. I was appalled to see used toilet paper had been swished along the toilet floor but not picked up. I had high hopes as the greeting staff were amazing, thank you Shannon.

We haven't even arrived yet but the staff over the phone have been so lovely and helpful with bookings and info, etc.  Nothing has been too much trouble.  If this is a hint of the service we will get when we arrive then we are sure we will have a wonderful time there.  We are really looking forward to our trip.

Great little park,we stayed on a powered site with concrete slab.|The amenities as in showers and toilets are very clean and the hot water excellent.|We didn't use the laundry but it looked in great order. $4 wash or dry.|The BBQs area and firepit both great for a sit around. We thought the lack of cooking as in stove top and oven not working wasn't real good but it was only for the night I am confident that will be rectified.|The staff when booking and checking in were outstanding, they couldn't do enough for you they were so kind friendly and hospitable.|Overall we enjoyed the stay. |Thank you
From the first phone call to driving in was such a great experience   this sort of service is what makes this country great 👍
Very tidy.
Needs to put in Wi fi  blinds need to be cleaned

We were here for a night on a powered slab. Friendly staff at the check-in. The site itself was fine and so were the amenities. The small size of the park probably helps to maintain a calm atmosphere here. We enjoyed our stay here.
Super Campingplatz

We came here for an overnight camping trip as a suprise for my husband's birthday and it was magical.

The pool is warm (perfect for cooler nights), the slides are ridiculously fun, theres a play ground, go karts, a pontoon on the creek (great for crabbing).

We had such a fantastic time and will be making this our regular family camping spot due to it's location and family-friendliness.

The facilities have kitchen, hot showers, toilets, rubbish, communal camp fire, food trucks (great for morning coffee),  restaurants (wasn't open when we came).
The staff have been amazing, looking after me during this very wet period of my week long stay.
So far so good
Lovely site, heated pool and 3 camp kitchens. Clean, modern facilities best site we stayed in
非常棒的营地 路面硬化度高 尘土很少 泳池很棒 餐厅好吃 还有送餐服务 camping和glamping体验都非常满意 一个月来玩了两次 还会再来
Nice tidy new park with great facilities, staff were awesome.
No unpowered camping next to river anymore. Under development apparently.

The area is nice, some things for kids to do. Shame the service around the area, with how staff treat guests is horrible. Not enough seating areas around the pool/playground area, abused if you sit in their eating area (even if you purchase food from them) Surcharge of 15% on any product purchased over a weekend. For nearly $200 a night I would expect staff to be a bit friendlier

We have had two visits to Rivershore. Our first visit we stayed in one of the Glamping tents and we absolutely loved it. They are spacious and have everything you need and they come with a Webber BBQ on the large deck which is also handy.

Our more recent visit last week was on a powered site in our caravan. We arrived and were placed on a “ Deluxe site” right next to all the works that are currently taking place. This actually didn’t bother me but a little communication / being advised at the time of booking / check in that we were going to be right next to noisy works would have been appreciated.

Our stay was during the school holidays so of course we are aware how busy etc it would be. They do need to be on top of how chaotic the pool is with the slides. We totally get it that kids are having fun but it is an accident waiting to happen. You have a staff member sometimes two at the top of the slides ( i am assuming to monitor the slides and let the next person know when they are safe to go) however this never occurred, the girls were spending more time on their phones rather than monitoring the safety. I had to take it upon myself so many times to ask the kids behind me to please wait until both myself and 2 year son were clear from the slide before flying down. Even after this it still didn’t happen and the amount of times we got hit after hitting the water by another kid was ridiculous.

The next issue they need to sort is the steps within the pool. They are the same colour as the actual pool and the amount of people either tripping or slipping off them in the pool was ridiculous. I know from our trip we aren’t the only ones unhappy with that. My partner knocked her legs many times and has been left with a number of proper bruises. All is required is to put a yellow line or some sort to show where the steps are. It is a simple safety hazard.

The female toilets had no hand soap for 3 days. This was fixed after sending a text message to the park asking for it to be fixed.

My last complaint here is probably one that needs to be addressed ASAP. The majority of the staff are so rude and just not interested. I absolutely hate to do this but one staff member in particular… HELEN, you need to be removed from the front reception. The way you speak to your guests and even kids is just not acceptable.

I enquired one evening about the advertised kids DISCO for my kids. You advised me it is not happening because the speaker was broken. Two nights later i enquired again and you told me “ read the sign, if its advertised then its on”  My reply to you was that i was advertised the other night and you cancelled because the speaker was broken and your reply was “ i think its fixed but I don’t really care as it is not me doing the disco tonight” … little things as simple as a disco is what some of the kids would actually really enjoy and yes the disco was on the second night but it isn’t much of a disco when you just place a speaker in the smallest corner.

The kids activities need to be better. So much potential but unfortunately the staff just have no interest. You pay good money to stay there in school holidays so you would at least expect some enthusiasm from the staff.

All in all not a bad park, just needs better staff and better improvements which can make the park a very good one.

Much better parks around though so do your research before booking this one.

Such a great spot for young families! My family of 4 spent 2 nights in one of the glamping tents, which were really nice.

Pros: Beautiful spot next to the river, great pool and slides, nice place to have a drink overlooking the pool, the hired karts were really fun and we enjoyed the free mini-golf too. Glamping tents were lovely with all amenities needed. Everyone really friendly and such a great atmosphere.

Cons:  Glamping tents very expensive and beds not very comfy, food limited at the cafe, with breakfast only served on weekends. Didn't sleep well due to sun pouring directly onto our faces in the main bed, and the campsite is quite noisy.

Overall, I would highly recommend a visit, especially for families with children  between 4-12 years. Lots to do!

So easy to get to. Reservations were easy.
Facilities are excellent. So much included. Pool was large and had slides. There are daily activities, such as archery, soccer games, movie nights, children's disco and animal encounters all included.
Glamping tent was huge with massive bathroom which included a bath. Beds were very comfortable. Outdoor settings and bar-b-que area are adjoined to the tent. Views are wonderful on the river, fishing spots all along.
Recommended!

We recently stayed in the Safari family tent. Absolutely stunning location, right next to the river. Beautiful big grassy area directly in front of our tent, ideal for the kids to play.

Beautiful spotless tent, huge king bed and 2 single (1 mattress for 3rd child set up!) in a separate curtained room.
Kitchenette ideal and included everything you need.
Bathroom is great, cute bath and great water pressure on the shower!
Deck and bbq area fab! Could’ve used a sink outside to clean the bbq but we made it work!
Cannot fault the accommodation in any way!

Activities for the kids to do, snake show, archery, wildlife show, disco, colouring competition, scavenger hunt - all at absolutely no extra cost!

Fire Pitt was such a good touch for roasting marshmallows!

Jumping pillow and putt putt we didn’t use as there was no shade in these areas for the kids.

Communal toilets and laundry - spotless

Play area - sand floor and shade made it perfect for the kids!

The pool area is amazing, clean, non stop fun on the 2 water slides for the kids, splash pool for the younger ones, shallow and deep areas in the big pool.

The only fault - I wish it wasn’t as this place had been faultless until the last few afternoons.

The pool, including sun loungers, cabana’s and play area can all be accessed by day guests for a fee (identified by a different wrist band to overnight guests) Being the Easter holidays the park is already full to the brim with the excited kids using the pool areas. The day guests, including big groups for birthday parties took over all the cabanas, loungers and filled the pool to the point of it being too busy and dangerous for our 3 young children. Unfortunately the parents of many of the older children (8+) were utilising the bar/cafe and not supervising their children. Bad language, wrestling with floaties, pushing and running were witnessed by our children, we also lost 2 floaties with kids just taking them! We had to pack up and leave the pool area on 2 occasions. When you are paying $580 a night, I feel it’s not good enough to have to leave because of day guests who have entered for a $20 fee.

We would return because the location and accommodation is amazing, we just would have reservations because of how crazy busy the pool area and seating became.

We stayed in the “Tradewind” Airstream caravan and were really excited for the novelty and “cool” factor of the experience. While it definitely has charm, the van itself feels quite dated and only partially renovated.
That said, it does include all the basic comforts you’d need—hot and cold running water, air conditioning, 240V power points, a TV, microwave, and a gas oven—so it’s still functional for a comfortable stay.
The highlight for us was the park itself. It’s a lovely place to stay, with amenities that feel relatively new and are generally kept clean—even during busy periods. We visited over the Easter weekend, and despite the crowds, the facilities were impressively well maintained.
Overall, a unique stay with a great location, but the Airstream could benefit from a more complete refresh.

Gorgeous location, amazing facilities and the staff are fantastic. My kids had the best time. Water slides for the big kids and splash pool for the littles. Playground, fire pits, fishing, pedal bikes and top tier service. It is also only about 15mins from tourist activities such as the big pineapple and sealife aquarium.

this was a lovely place for us, all friendly people, very helpful staff. all amenities where spotless, fresh and clean. lots to do for the kids, great pool and excellent food in the restaurant. we have to move on further up to Cairns, but we could easy stay another couple of days.
Great place if you have kids with plenty of activities for the  and the pool is great for everyone, love the bar and other activities that the park arranges and keeps you informed about
Well maintained clean site with excellent amenities. Highly recommend!

Currently staying and the facilities and staff are fantastic.

The ladies on reception were a delight to deal with

- very nice caravan park to stay for a few days or longer
- good location for day trips to surrounding locations
- nice food, good drinks
- clean pool with cool slides
- very helpful and lovely staff

Wow what a fantastic place, from the moment we booked in, so friendly, accomodation, amenities was spot on. Pool was so much fun for all the kids and adults. Went out of there way to accommodate my daughter and grandies while we had torrential rain.
Amazing holiday! Staff were super friendly and helpful! Perfect location. We had the family safari tent was a great size and was very clean. Will definitely be returning for another great holiday.

Stayed here last night. What a fantastic place. Everything about this is just spot on. The sites - lots of choice from off grid on the riverfront to luxury glamping and everything g in between. The pool with slides and a toddler pool - amazing! The facilitate clean and well thought out. Ample of everything - amenities, kitchens, bins, etc. The cafe/restaurant has great food and food & drinks are reasonably priced.   The staff are friendly and on the ball.
In all, highly recommend.
Craig was the best at both our stays nearly a year apart. His energetic, warm energy made it such a great time. The kids will never forget their favourite bar man .

Absolute paradise for families. A great location by a lake good for fishing, stunning views of sunsets and sunrise. Slides are free and are great fun! The service is top tier. Fun activities such as pedal karts, mini golf and petting zoo. The facilities inside the glamp are extremely comfy and premium. There are 3 beds, a bath, and a barbecue. There is a fireplace, perfect for toasting marshmallows and more. One of the best holiday parks ever!
Rivershore was a great place to camp. The pool, restraunt and bar is fantastic. The kids loved the pool and slides, mini golf, jumping pillow and the movie nights. Would definitely come back again.

Stayed on a powered site in a tent and was immediately taken aback by the fact we were literally right next to a very busy road. It was hard to sleep at night and we were woken by the sounds of traffic in the early hours.
The amenities were good and the kids really enjoyed the pool. Reception could benefit from being warmer - I felt like a huge bother to them by asking some simple questions.

Great little place. Good pool for kids. Bar service slow (got our food before the drinks arrived).
Good spot :)

We've stayed at another Ingenia site and it was not the same experience. We stayed here for 6 nights in a Safari tent (glamping). For the amount we paid approx ($2,800) for a tent, it was not best value for money.

Pros:
- Location
- Nice river view
- Nice pool with slides for kids
- Little to no mosquito's

Cons:
- Tent needed a bit more servicing given we stayed for 6 days
- VERY noisy at night time with kids running around well past 9pm and adults having loud conversations on thier balconies. The tents are built very close on either sides.
- We flew in from Sydney, so relying on a well sourced kitchen for utensils was key for cooking on a bbq. We needed to purchase a frypan as the grill did not have a flat hotplate. We could not use the common BBQ due to food allergies.
- Children running and riding around without supervision at all hours (not the fault of Ingenia), its a serious issue, when trying to navigate in a car to your tent at 10 kph and having to stop to ensure kids are not being run over.
- The tent needed a DEEP clean, the floor felt grimey from day 1. My Husband found a pair of undies behind the TV unit which tells me cleaning is not to standard (refer photo).
- The front reception staff could be a bit more welcoming and informative.

Absolutely loved camping with my family last weekend.  Great location. Riverfront access, ideal for fishing. Swimming Pool and waterslides were a huge hit. Great location for a perfect family weekend away. Highly recommend 👌

We stayed here over Christmas week 2025. It was our first time at the park and couldn't have been more pleased with our stay. The staff were so lovely and thoughtful. We booked a powered site for our 9m caravan and the site was huge. Could have fit another caravan and car on it. Facilities were amazing for the kids with a full school holiday planner of activities. Everything was well cleaned and serviced, down to the wheely bins being emptied regular so no smell. We compared it to being on a cruise ship, the only thing missing was the kids club 😊 Will definitely be back to this park 10/10
The staff were amazing and they went above and beyond to make our stay nice and joyful.

Positiiviset:
- Lapsiystävällinen ja viihtyisä paikka
- Hiljainen yöllä
- Vessat olivat siistit
- Tiivis yhteisö

Negatiiviset:
- Kaukana kaikesta

I attempted to book a powered site for one night during school holidays. I could clearly see online that a one-night stay was available, however I was having trouble with the payment and called the park to book instead.

The woman I spoke to was extremely rude, abrupt and completely unwilling to help. She immediately told me I could not book for one night, despite it being available online. When I explained that I was only calling because the online system wasn’t working for me and I didn’t want to pay extra for flexible cancellation, she snapped back, “I’d rather you just book online then!”

I explained again that I was trying to do exactly that, but was having trouble. She then spoke to me in a very harsh and dismissive tone and flatly refused to book a one-night stay at all.

As a result of her attitude and refusal to help, they lost my business. I called another caravan park just down the road who were friendly, accommodating and happy to book us in for one night.

Customer service like this is unacceptable. I won’t be staying here and would not recommend this park based on the way I was spoken to
A great place for the kids, you have everything here just put your swag up and enjoy.

Very relaxing & mellow place!
Even got to watch UFC on the tv! And internet is best out of any holiday park we’ve been to and all staff were great from house keeping to room service to reception. Only thing missing is spa pool and maybe sauna but that’s me being picky :)

We have loved our stay at Rivershore.
The staff are so lovely and very helpful. The facilities are awesome, very clean and well kept.  We have stayed in the safari tents and they are amazing. The tents have everything you need comfy bed, aircon, a tv, kitchenette, bathroom (with a shower and bath) and a Webber out on your balcony. This is a yearly visit for us.

Special thanks to the amazing team at Rivershore! Front desk you are just beautiful people; always helpful and polite!
Thank you for helping us when we needed to extend our stay!
Jean you are a treasure to the group they are lucky to have you as their manager!
Craig your empathy towards the visitors and animals alike is truly amazing!
Miss you guys!
By far the Best caravan park resort along the Sunshine Coast. The team are so friendly and helpful. Grounds are spectacular and well manicured. Craig at the restaurant is the best. Thanks team.

Beautiful spot. Camped in camper trailer right on the river. BYO power and water on these sites. Easy access boat ramp. Nice pool with slids. Heaps of room. Paddle boarding the creek was easy. Will definitely return.

We give our stay a 4.8.
We loved our staycation and definitely will be back soon. As a kayaker, being right on the river is magical. Staying in a Safari tent is our kind of camping!  Great facilities, and the staff were all exceptional. We were granted a late check out as well, which was much appreciated.
It would normally be a 5 star rating. The only tiny complaint was that, although booking Driftwood Restaurant early, for 6.30pm, it closed unexpectantly early. We had friends visiting for my birthday dinner, so that was a little disappointing. Otherwise, all was brilliant. Thank you to all!
Warmest regards
Maike & Paul
Yandina

I have stayed here previously and the staff were very friendly. This stay was good except for the lady at reception did not ask how my stay was just abused me for driving over the concrete slab when I was just trying to my car and caravan off road. I would not return to park if this is who I had to deal with. Kind regards Andrew
Love this place but the menu in driftwood is a let down. I recommend the park but eat across the road at the pub.

I have just celebrated my 40th birthday at Ingenia Rivershore and the staff were absolutely amazing in making sure the whole process and event ran very smoothly. The staff went above and beyond for all my guests and myself and we couldn’t be happier. Thanks again team Rivershore. See you again soon 💕

We had a wonderful time at Rivershore Camping Site. The location is secure and conveniently situated, making it easy to explore and enjoy the beautiful surroundings. The staff were friendly and helpful, creating a warm and welcoming atmosphere throughout our stay.

The water park was a fantastic highlight — fun for both kids and adults, and the café served delicious food that we thoroughly enjoyed. We also spent time fishing by the lake and hired bikes to explore the area.

The shared cooking facilities were well-equipped, and the toilets and showers were always clean and well-maintained.

Overall, Rivershore Camping Site offers the perfect combination of comfort, convenience, and great hospitality. It’s a wonderful place to relax, have fun, and enjoy nature. Highly recommended! We'll be back.
Absolutely loved my stay here. A big shout out to Jett for his outstanding service for us.  He deserves to be recognised for a job well done and appreciated by us all.

Fabulous large unpowered sites along the river. Site W15 has the best views. However, I heard that the unpowered riverfront sites will soon be turned into glamping & cabin sites once Council has approved it (possibly Jan 2026)

My first time with the kids, my partner had come before with our daughters and her friends. We had our youngest, who had been counting down the sleeps until the slide park, to be awfully treated. We waited for entry, my partner going in to secure a spot. When I got to the service lady, she so rudely told me they were booked out and didn't I see the sign! No i didn't see a sign and I lined up with everyone else. I politely explained my partner had secured a spot and she more aggressively told me again that the sign says we are booked out, then looked at my 11 year old and 5 year old and said" you can't come in!" "Someone is monitering wristbands so im sure he will be kicked out soon". My partner then asked the other lady on duty if there was any room and she tried and was very empathic when the other lady returned and said "I already told them no" The nice lady then booked us in for tomorrow and said come early as i feel it was compensation for the other womens rude service. My kids call her the really rude lady. Extremely disappointing and so unnecessary, she could have it explained it to me nicely, something you expect from customer service. I would definitely have a chat to that staff member, remembering we are paying locals with young kids that love your venue.

Everyone has been so accommodating to us in our extra large bendy bus. The bar staff and reception staff are very helpful and kind. There isn't really any need to leave the camp grounds with fishing, swimming, bouncing castle and go karts to keep everyone happy!
We absolutely love it. The bed was comfortable, space is great. Staff were amazing and food was exceptional. Will definitely be back.
This place is the best! Staff amazing, sites large, amenities clean and modern.

We are local and visit this beautiful place frequently. The amenities and activities are great and the management staff are incredibly friendly and generous and always make our time and experience there a great one.

Absolutely love this place. Got married here and always love coming up from the Gold Coast to stay here. Great location, huge sites, great amenities and the staff are always friendly and helpful. Resort style pool that the kids spend all day having fun.

Our family holiday at Rivershore Ingenia was an absolute dream come true!

From the moment we arrived, the staff were amazing — so friendly, welcoming, and always happy to help. The location is perfect, surrounded by nature and ideal for families.

There’s so much to do — from relaxing by the pool, playing mini golf, fishing, hair braiding, and hours of fun on the bouncing pillow. The kids never stopped smiling, and neither did we!
Everything was clean, well-organised, and designed to make family holidays effortless and fun. We honestly didn’t want to leave. Highly recommend Rivershore Ingenia — the perfect spot for unforgettable family memories!

Such a beautiful camping spot with everything you need, but what really makes it stand out is the staff. They are so welcoming, friendly, and go out of their way to make you feel at home. It’s rare to find a place where the people make such a difference to your stay. Can’t wait to come back.

Fantastic place. Great amenities, kids playground,mini golf and giant jumping pillow. The swimming pool and slide were a favourite for the kids and the private cabanas were a nice touch.
The food at the restaurant was lovely and not to mention the great cocktails at happy hour.
Nice open spaces along the river for larger RV set-ups.

Great Caravan Park
Dog Friendly
Cafe, Bar ,Happy Hour
Very relaxing

We absolutely loved this park. Warm water pool with a lap swimming lane in the deep end. Grass on the caravan site slabs. Clean, modern amenities (the shower heads were great). The roads were very well maintained, as were the vast lawns. The large sites, even for our 30ft caravan made for an easy fit. The park isn't too big and never felt crowded. The staff were lovely too. You'll see us again.
Great amenities, staff were Awesome, well worth the high price!

I celebrated my big birthday here and the staff were absolutely wonderful. Managed to group 9 large campsites together and we had the most fabulous time. Jean on front counter was.exceedingly accommodating and made our stay just brilliant. We stayed 5 nights and hardly left the camp ground. Lovely facilities, heated pool, happy hour and meal specials..the kids fished off the pontoon for hours. Even the teenagers didn't want to leave camp. I cannot wait to go back!

From the moment we checked in the service was amazing,  warming and ever so welcoming. The staff are extremely helpful and friendly and the grounds are immaculate. Couldn't have asked for anything more than what was provided. Thankyou for making our holiday memorable 💖...
Love from the chubby women from Melbourne that loves to swear ✨️😎
Stayed in our own camper. Beautiful big sites. Lots of amenities buildings. Great pool area and had Dinner twice with great meal and service at on-site restaurant. Thankyou

Great and roomy caravan park. Facilities and ammenities are very modern and clean. Great glamping tents. Restaurant, bar and pool areas are comfortable and attractive surrounds. Food great. Service friendly and prompt. Lots happening and I love the frequent text invitations to what’s on. Thanks staff. Lovely place.
Great caravan park close to all major attractions and shops,one of the best we have stayed in. I
Our group went to the restaurant for a meal.
Cool place to chill out with dinner and a Margarita

Great park. Both powered and unpowered sites are great.
Staff went out of their way to help us when it rained non stop, drove us to bus stop and no hesitation in moving us to drier site.
Amenities clean, showered had great water pressure.
Definitely recommend.

We stayed in the family safari tent in August. What a great set up! Keeping the heat pump on kept us warm all night. Loved the location, the staff were amazing and the rooms were excellent. Hands down the most comfy beds I've slept in. I loved the claw foot bath tub too.
The kids loved the pool and slides. The pedal carts were hit too. I loved the bird life and waking to the bird song every morning.

Only complaint was that the beds had flat sheets, not fitted and the sheets kept coming away and tangling up.
We'd be happy to come back and stay again

Fantastic place.
Staff were excellent and go out of there way to help you.
Will definitely be back.

Not a bad park pretty price, some over keen management has spread gravel all over the place which has ruined the grass on the powered sites. Also the mens showers near the pool have the water running out the cubicles making the floor slippery and dangerous. A lovely lady in the office relocated away from the pool pump shed on request.
Very surprised. Really nice

We have just spent 9 nights at Rivershore and it would be one of the best caravan parks we have ever stayed in.  My husband took ill on Sunday and was taken to hospital.  The care and concern that was shown to me by Jean and Aleshia was beyond any expectation I had.  Will certainly recommend Rivershore as the caravan park to stay when in Maroochydore.
A hidden gem.  One of the best camp sites I have ever been to.  If you have kids it is a must with so much to do.

Beware not all Deluxe sites are equal, 40 and 51 are directly opposite the children’s playground and where the food trucks park, loud music from the pool area all day.  If that’s what you want book these if you want something quieter book alongside the field, we did but were advised we hadn’t requested them. Not sure we’ll pay that much again to stay. Shame we’ve previously had a nice time here!

Nice tents and caravan park. With lots of facilities like pool, laundry, tea/coffee maker, kitchen and kitchenette. River side view, friendly staff and good restaurant. Kids can spend whole day without boring, having movie night for kids , kids disco, kids pool, camp fire. common laundry room and kitchen with refrigerator for camping people. BBQ facility
One of the best holidays ever great place ,great people. Highly recommended.
Good location but not really a standout for Ingenia
Great location, clean, neat and tidy. Good restaurant on-site.
Awesome park with friendly staff and great amenities for adults and kids.
Every time we visit the Sunny Coast we receive the very service and I want to give a shout out to lovely lady on the front counter today and Libby behind bar who makes spectacular cocktails

Unreal spot! We just finished up a 3 night stay here. Great big spacious powered sites along the pool side. Park and jumping pillow for the kids as well as a shallow kiddie pool/splash park. Plenty of fun for the big kids with a big heated pool and water slides.Pool side drink service was a big plus. Sites were well maintained. Staff were super friendly and helpful in helping us stay an extra night when we didn't want to leave so soon.

The riverside non powered sites looked amazing also!

Definitely stay here again was the most fantastic family holiday park we have ever stayed at friendly staff the grounds are well kept pool and slides are awesome the amenities such as laundry are clean and easy to use , we stayed in the safari tent its the best thing I’ve ever seen and stayed in such a cool experience furniture is top notch everything clean so much room and had everything you could possibly need also backs onto river easy access for fishing
Great central location. Awesome selection of tasty food and reasonably priced. Look out for the weekly specials!!!! Great bar and selection of beers etc.
Call in for coffee and lunch what great staff great coffee and great food

To the girl behind the cafe counter this morning around 9am, you are an exceptional asset and thank you for trying to keep the customer line moving forward in order. She obviously has great customer service skills. Thanks again

We recently stayed in one of the glamping tents at this holiday resort and came away with mixed feelings. Having stayed here before and thoroughly enjoyed it, we were looking forward to another relaxing experience—but unfortunately, this visit didn’t quite live up to expectations.||The positives are still there: the tent was absolutely beautiful—well-designed, spotless, and very comfortable. The surrounding grounds remain as lovely as ever, offering a peaceful natural setting and thoughtful touches that make the resort visually appealing.||However, the tranquility we had hoped for was disrupted by a couple of key issues. Noise from the nearby main road was quite noticeable, especially during the night. In addition, some of the guests staying nearby played loud music, which really impacted the relaxing atmosphere and made it hard to unwind.||This resort has so much potential, and we know from past experience that it can be a truly peaceful getaway. With better noise control and clearer expectations for guest behaviour, it could easily return to being the restful escape it once was.

Amazing camping venue by the creek. Although we stayed in our swags it had a resort feel. The showers and all facilities were first class.

The boat ramp was very handy also. We will be back on day.
Great little place to camp and stay. Host's absolutely fantastic. Very layed back. Losts Of animals to see.
Great little caravan park. Friendly staff and in a really good location to Nambour

We all look for budget accommodation at times but this was ridiculous.One of the worst experiences of cheap accommodation we have ever had , cabin was old  and run down , full of cobwebs and spiders inside and bugs were in every corner of the cabin and everything we wanted to use we had to clean as it seemed nobody else had prepared it for new visitors.Nights were far worse after being woken up by bugs an cockroaches crawling across my face. Would not recommend for any traveller looking for budget accommodation. Would have better accommodation in a tent. Is there a star with a minus on the review???P.s reminder to myself: check all reviews on any budget accommodation !!!! Only gave 1star because I had to .
Becoming very run down, not sure if this park would pass safety audit. Current manager very friendly and helpful, mostly permanents. Just a shame about its Current condition.

Have traveled all around Australia with our van, unfortunately this van park has been the worst!! So much potential but seriously neglected, toilets blocked constantly??? Paid for 7 nights only stayed 3 never bothered to ask for a refund didn’t see the Managers during the duration, van site was the remains of an old building concrete slab was split level!! Extremely disappointing

Had a great stay at Rainforest holiday village, clean amenities and nice camp kitchen to chill. Close to Cole’s and Aldi and not far from Maroochydore, beaches and hinterland areas.
Met owner of business who was friendly and spoke to managers at pool area who were good with advice on what to do locally! Lots of shade and tonnes of lizards and bush turkeys. Will be back one day again.

We stayed in this caravan park for 6 months. The new caretakers were amazing, the cleaned up the park all the time of branches, rubbish (hard) nothing was ever an issue.
Rates for caravan  park were nice and reasonable, we had a big site with awesome  neighbours.
Toilet block is old but functional
Nil issues staying there at all.
If we were to go back to the Sunshine  Coast would certainly  stay at this park again
Excellent little hideaway. Real rainforest.

After travelling around Oz in our motorhome,( three times), this park needs so much tlc, it is neglected. Needs so much $ spent to bring it up to scratch. Worse park we have ever stayed.  The  showers just fill up and you stand in water over the top of thongs, pressure is minimal. Only good thing the water is hot. Don’t book here, the cabins don’t look much better.

Unfortunately not a place for families. We wish we had been told that this campsite was not child friendly before booking. Many elderly people live here (we did not know this) and do not tolerate any noise whatsoever. Some extreme religious people also live here and accosted me in the kitchen about wanting a wife!! We stayed over the long weekend and our children were just being kids - excited to use the pool and park and we were abused for them making noise between the hours of 8am and 5pm. Our dog accidentally got off her leash and walked 2 meters in front of us in which we ran after her and leashed her immediately. The manager aggressively told me that if it happened again he would put her in the pound and said he will be watching us on his “cameras”. I was crying and apologising profusely but he was rude, smug and unwilling to have a civil conversation over a very minor incident. I also approached the other campers and apologised for the children making noise. Many were good about it and appreciated the gesture but many also explained that this is not a place for kids and they don’t want any noise at all. I understand this but it’s up to management to notify people of this before booking. Due to the hostility, religious nuts telling us we will go to hell if we don’t repent and requesting wife “duties”, and being completely in child friendly, we felt we had no choice but to leave. They are refusing to refund us for the nights we couldn’t stay which is their prerogative but displays an attitude and business practice of poor customer service. Internet did not work either. I gave two stars because the Bush land is pretty, does have all facilities needed for a budget stay and would suit a very quiet single person. I have camped and hiked in many different place with and without children and this is the worst treatment I have ever come across. We did everything we could to contain our children and unfortunately they spent most of the time on screens inside our camper as we were all afraid of the people running the place and our beloved pet being taken away.
EDIT: Refund finally given but the response to our review and experience is an utter lie.

A long way below par. Filthy amenities and in bad need of a facelift everywhere. The only positive is a good location. Don’t believe a word from this website.could not recommend to any traveller.Management were no where to be seen.|This is not a holiday park at all.
Sanitaire très vieillot. Chère pour la qualité donné.

We booked two nights at the park. We arrived to a very tight site with water and mud all around it. We noticed a lot of permed ants and felt eyes on us from the permanents that we were not welcome. One woman walk past clearly off her face having no idea where she was. We were there ten mins, went into town, which was also awful, dark and unwelcoming. Decided we didn’t feel safe, we wouldn’t leave out a blade of grass! Packed up and went to bli bli caravan park, it’s fabulous. Waiting our refund from the rainforest. Very old, dary and tight parking.

Lovely Rainforest setting. Caravan site is on the smaller side compared to other van parks but still large enough for van and car. Amenities are old but spotless. Booking was easy. Some wildlife critters, lizards, bush turkeys around the sites.

I stayed here for 4 nights in early February, we rang the park in evening and got a spot for the next day. large sites to accommodate most big caravans, it was easy check in system, pool was crystal clear and amenities were spotless, new manager was friendly and informative about the local area. Palm trees everywhere and so much birds. Located walking distance to Nambour to supermarkets and shops and also 15 or so minutes to central Maroochydore where we went to cotton tree beach and Alexander beach surf club for dinner, on the 2nd night we went to Woombye pub which is close to the park. Great pub with nice food. So nice 😊 we will return, thank you. C#

New owners have kicked out old maintenance man who always kept the grounds tidy and rubbish bins delivered back so we could dispose of rubbish. I’m a long term tenant and would have appreciated notification about new owner's but it is what it is. There are no birds in the cages anymore. Maybe last owner took them but it was nice having birds there for new people to visit. Laundry room no longer has operating washing machines so you have to take it elsewhere. Very inconvenient. Grateful that the old owner let me and my son stay here as housing is difficult to attain. Overall, place is kid friendly as many families live here. Well wishes to the old landlord and management. Hoping new owners will do better or at least be on par. 🙏🏽
Very poor establishment to get in touch with. Would not answer phone and could not book on line.
Brilliant service, bar staff couldn’t do more for you

Stayed the weekend at this CP and had a great stay.  Went to Happy Hour both days and we were very impressed with Craig the manager of food and beverage. Very hands on and excellent service from the staff
Thanks Craig for the personalised service. You made us feel special and at home. Coming from Perth WA you made us feel like we were the only people in the restaurant. Highly recommend this place.

Enjoyed our stay on a powered site at times the amenities were a little untidy as I only seen them cleaning them once a day during the morning , unfortunately the pool was closed for maintenance throughout our stay however there was plenty of other things to do. The restaurant onsite makes delicious food and beverages.

We highly recommend this caravan park! The grounds are beautifully maintained, the location is stunning, and the staff are incredibly friendly and accommodating.
Everything was perfect  we couldn’t fault a single thing. We’ll definitely be spreading the word and recommending it to everyone we know.
Thank you so much for making our long weekend such an enjoyable and memorable stay!

My husband and I stayed in a safari tent right in front of the river. It was peaceful with a beautiful view and fishing spot. The room was tidy and absolutely perfect for winter—even though the nights were seriously cold. I'm 19 weeks pregnant, so I was a bit worried about the facilities, but now I'm actually looking forward to coming back with our newborn baby.
Thank you to the team for helping us make such wonderful memories.
Great park

Unser Lieblings Campingplatz an der Sunshine Coast!
Es ist alles tip top, sehr sauber und alles sehr modern. Es gibt einen großen Pool und ein Restaurant.
Am Wochenende leider recht teuer, aber da fahren wir immer nach Noosa.
Ist ne 10/10!
Great park awesome staff sites are big every thing is well maintained and an awesome pool
Fantastics facilities topped with a restaurant & Cafe &  Bar while watching kids in pool

The customer service line is unanswered after 7 calls.  Emails are not returned.
Socially media continually joins you for bookings and payment.

Cannot cancel.
VERY POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE.

We cannot wait to go back. The park and amenities were immaculate and the pool, bar and restaurant are amazing. The sites are a good size, we stayed on a powered site and had plenty of room. Also, the staff are very friendly and helpful.
Good facilities, great location but amazing staff. Been here so many times and always left with a smile.

Can't comment in rooms as we have our own van, but what an amazing place. Currently staying here and can't wait to come back. Lovely and quiet, great for fishing and kayaking. Pool with a bar what more could you want. Oh and if you have kids, heaps for them to be involved in.

We are local and have been here a few times and despite the lovely and accommodating new managers the standards and attention to detail have really lapsed. The stairs on the slide could be gurneyed, as could the pool slide rules, cobwebs on back of toilet doors, weeds near cabanas and blood on female toilet doors have been there for days. I recommend regular cleaning and grounds inspections as it isn’t maintained as well as it used to be. However I would like to say a big thankyou to Alicia who ensured we extended our stay and didn’t have to move sites. She is lovely and you can’t tell she really enjoys her job!

The best swimming - playground - bar layout we’ve seen in a caravan park. Perfect for families with kids. Dog friendly. Very clean and modern facilities. We stayed in the basic powered site which did the job, but would consider the unpowered riverside sites next time.

The staff are absolutely wonderful, the facilities are clean & tidy + the kitchenette & laundry were a handy bonus.
Thank you, Fay & Peter, for everything
Great place to stay great amenities very clean nothing was to much trouble for Fay and Peter and other staff highly recommended staying there
Cleanest amenities in any caravan park I've seen. Very welcoming, great two night stay, close to Australia Zoo 😄

We had a great six weeks here. The facilities are very clean … toilets, showers, laundry and kitchen. There is undercover seating and cooking facilities. Faye and Peter will go out of their way to help you with anything you need. Thank you for a great stay.
Didnt have a booking but managed to get us in the last slot. Very friendly, offering ample advice on what to do in the area. Cant fault facilities either
Having stayed for two nights. Very clean facilities everything you need. A late booking Fay the owner couldn’t do enough for us. Everyone friendly. Peaceful place.  Definitely recommend

The campsite is beautiful, very well maintained, and spotless. The hosts, Pete and his mother Fay, are incredibly friendly and welcoming. You can tell they really know what they’re doing and have a lot of experience. They take great care of everything and make sure guests feel completely comfortable. Truly one of the best places! Thank you so much! Cheers Rebecca and Joel

Nice little camp, very private wirh a big gate getting in and going out. Mostly are permanent campers but very friendly people, everyone was nice. Nice and tidy amenities. Only far from everything. Nice and quite place. We fou s it gets really warm in our campervan when sun is out because its in the open, not much shade.

We had such a lovely stay here and honestly didn’t want to leave. Fay and Pete are absolutely wonderful hosts so friendly welcoming and always up for a chat and always happy to help. They really go out of their way to make you feel at home. Everything was super clean and peaceful which made it so easy to relax and switch off. The whole place has such a calm friendly vibe we felt like family.
If I'm every back in the area would  definitely stay again

couldn’t recommend it more. 5 stars
Cracking little spot. Very friendly service and nice clean facilities. Close to Australia Zoo which was very handy for us. Would definitely stay again next time we are in the area.

Great location for visiting the Glasshouse Mountains and the Australia Zoo. It doesn't have a pool but it's Dog friendly and the amenities are spotlessly clean.
Fay,(Manager), does a great job and is very knowledgeable and helpful.
Nice van park tucked away off Steve Irwin way, just a few km out of Beerwah. Handy for zoo visits and staying around the hinterland area.  Stopped in here to catch up with family in the area.
Quiet Caravan park with lovely hosts, always trying to help their visitors. Amenities include shower, toilets, fully equipped kitchen, access to power and drinking water. Would definetly come back.

Such an amazing caravan site, the staff truly make the experience and it feels like such a community when you arrive. The site is extremely well maintained, with hot showers and spacious sites to park and 8 minutes away from Australia Zoo. I wouldn’t hesitate to stay again!

Sehr schöner und gepflegter Campingplatz, der wirklich alles bietet, was man für einen angenehmen Aufenthalt braucht. Besonders positiv aufgefallen sind uns die sehr sauberen und gut gepflegten Duschen und Toiletten, dass ist auf Campingplätzen nicht immer selbstverständlich. Man merkt, dass hier viel Wert auf Sauberkeit und Ordnung gelegt wird.

Wir waren zwar nur eine Nacht hier, hatten aber eine wirklich tolle Zeit und haben unseren Aufenthalt sehr genossen. Die Atmosphäre auf dem Platz ist ruhig, entspannt und sehr angenehm.

Ein ganz besonderes Dankeschön geht an Fay. Sie ist eine wunderbare Frau, unglaublich freundlich, hilfsbereit und mit einem grossen Herzen. Man fühlt sich sofort willkommen und gut aufgehoben. Ihre herzliche Art macht den Aufenthalt gleich noch schöner. Ich kann die ganzen negativen Kommentare gar nicht nachvollziehen.

Wir würden jederzeit wiederkommen und können diesen Campingplatz von Herzen weiterempfehlen.
What a gem. Faye and staff are super friendly and helpful. The facilities are spotless, we would definitely stay there again
Quiet clean spacious with new clean bathroom and shower facilities.
Good facilities, right on the river.

This is a very lovely place to stay, clean, and friendly staff, that look after you,  the grounds are well kept.Toilets kept clean. The pool area gives a very resort style feeling.
We really enjoyed our stay and wanted to stay longer honestly one of the best parks we’ve been to .

I organized a surprise week at Rivershore for my parents 50th wedding anniversary and their 70th birthdays. Family and friends traveled from Victoria and other parts of qld to stay and celebrate. Jean and her staff went above and beyond to keep this surprise from my parents, who kept ringing trying to check in early and ruin the surprise! Jean was in constant contact when guests arrived to ensure the staff were not giving the surprise away and to make sure it all went off without a hitch. It did! ||There were 21 of us in various accommodation types and nothing was too much for the staff to accommodate us. From delivering groceries to the glamping tents so my cousin and kids were in place for the big surprise, to providing extra beds when our tent got flooded! ||On check in I was gifted pedal cart vouchers for the kids and a cocktail voucher for me! On check out my parents were personally gifted wine, chocolates and a Rivershore hat from Jean to congratulate them on their milestones and thank them for staying at Rivershore. ||It was torrential rain and wind all week yet we still swam, played mini golf, cricket, board games, used the water slides, did water aerobics, roasted marshmallows and made family memories we won't forget. Especially my Mum and Dad's reaction to that surprise! Thank you so much Jean and staff, it means more than I can say ||I highly recommend Rivershore, all accommodation options are amazing with something to suit everyone. The staff are happy and treat each other and guests like family. The facilities and location are perfect for all ages and it is a wonderful holiday in any weather.

Enjoyed staying four nights in a motorhome. The weather was not perfect but we  managed two days cycling . Two days walking and catching the bus. Rivershore to Noosa for lunch 50cents bus trip with no parking problems !
The facilities are really good pool was great . I appreciated the time the staff take to keep the verandah area dry during the rain . Coffee and cake / cakes not to be missed .
