Bar and dining service great . Staff are friendly and helpful. Amazing cleaners who kept the bathrooms dry and clean . No puddles under the awning the.  Artificial grass pads are great . Well done team

What a fantastic resort!!! Honestly, this is one of the best parks we have stayed at. We were supposed to go elsewhere for 2 nights but due to the intense rain, we changed our trip and ended up here. I can honestly say we were very happy everything changed.||We spent 2 nights in a King Safari Tent, special mid week rate. Cot and high chair were setup by the time we arrived. Talk about glamping, this was a beautiful setup. Air con, full bathroom with free standing bath, shower, toilet all separate to bedroom. Tv, coffee & tea making, BBQ on the deck overlooking the river.||Whilst we didn’t use the laundry facilities scattered around the park we had a quick browse and noticed there were quite a few, plenty of machines available, laundry powder, games machines etc. The pools were also excellent, our 11 month old loved the child’s pool and even though I’m a little old for the slides, you could clearly see the older kids loving them!||We ate pretty much every meal at the restaurant/bar. Every time the food was great! Well priced, fresh, tasty and pretty fast. The real shining light to this place though was the staff. Every single person we met was extremely friendly and just happy to help in any way possible. A real credit to the business, well done everyone!

We have only just arrived a few hours ago and are super impressed, a group booking for a 50th surprise wedding anniversary. The team here have gone all out to ensure it went smoothly, even upgrading our booking as we had booked a room on the other side of the park away from the main group oops!! We are suitably impressed with the glamping tent!! It has a claw foot bath and a massive and very comfortable bed!!
Lunch was delicious, I can’t wait to see what the next few days bring!!

Perfect location in the middle of theme Sunshine Coast with easy access to the highway to get around. We used this spot as base camp for a weekend of kids sports. Very glad we did. Pool and restaurant are constantly packed though, and they allow outside visitors to pay to use the facilities. At $250+ for a caravan site the weekend and then not really behind able to get a seat at the restaurant or a spot / chair by the pool it felt a little disappointing. The park was full this weekend + another 20-30 car of visitors it’s over capacity for enjoyable use of facilities. The sites are big, but the roads are tight to maneuver through as they have wooden posts to save the grass, make the corners wider could prevent the grass from being run over. It’s a hell of a lot of space that  as been a little Jammed in.

Overall, it’s a really nice place, and clearly just a super busy weekend here. We will absolutely come back and book a waterfront site again.

We recently had the most magical experience at Ingenia Rivershore for our wedding, and it truly exceeded all our expectations. From the moment we arrived, we were blown away by the exceptional service that made our stay unforgettable. We spent the morning getting ready in the beautiful glamping tents, which were nothing short of stunning—comfortable, elegant, and a perfect setting to start our special day.

A huge shoutout to Laetitia, who went above and beyond to ensure our experience was seamless and memorable. Upon check-in, I was greeted with such a thoughtful gesture—chocolates, wine, and roses in our tent. It brought tears to my eyes and reminded me of how rare and meaningful it is to experience such personalized, old-school service in today's world.

It’s also so clear that the staff at Ingenia Rivershore genuinely enjoy working together. The way everyone interacts with one another shows a true sense of team spirit and unity. It truly feels like one big family, which makes the entire experience even more special. Every member of the team, from reception to the restaurant, housekeeping, and grounds crew, went out of their way to make our stay perfect.

Our wedding reception at the Driftwood Restaurant was a dream come true; the atmosphere was intimate, beautiful, and complemented by the impeccable service. We honestly felt like family throughout our stay, and we can't wait to share this experience with everyone we know. A massive thank you to everyone who made our wedding day so special. We’ll definitely be back!

Loved our stay .
Large van sites and a great pool .

What a fabulous place for families! We had about 14 people - various ages from 3 - 69 years stay for a 3-night stay. Caravaning, glamp tenting etc. It was a really wonderful and affordable holiday. The cost of drinks, food, ice blocks etc - was so reasonable. One night we went to the Waterfront Hotel which was a 7-minute walk, and the food was great. Some of the gardens and fixtures around the place require some maintenance before it gets a bit too much further. All the staff were incredible, except for one lady who became irritated with me when I complained about a dog locked inside a van and it hadn't stopped barking. Very likely that we will stay again.

Rivershore was an awesome place to stay, staff were very friendly and extra helpful 😀.
The manager was just as lovely!
Kids enjoyed the waterside in the pool and caught some fish in the river.
We will definitely be back ⛰️

What a great spot! The distance to town is roughly 5-10 mins drive but the location of being out of town is blissful and makes it worth it. Pool side, deluxe site in a van: perfection. ||Positives: Good staff; in particular Alisha! She was the best! She went over and above for our family. Other positives included good facilities, great pool area, good for kids of all ages, so including (go karts, mini putt putt, bounce pillow, soccer, water park, pool, slides, playground and best of all open grass!). Another positive, the parent rooms are such a great idea! We had 3 to pick from, which were also unisex / disabled toilets and this was great. We used these every day! ||Negative: The facilities via the pool are not well kept, as they could be. We were made aware on arrival that this is the bathrooms day visitors so obviously they get used a lot, so it makes sense.

Ok not cheap (we were on a powered site)..  but you do get good, helpful staff, great location, well maintained grounds and amenities. The drinks are reasonably priced and there's happy hour. There is a restaurant and it's reasonably priced.

Ein kleiner sehr sauberer ruhiger, abgelegener Campingplatz. Die Toiletten und Duschen sind  sehr sauber.
Peter ist einfach nur nett freundlich und Hilfsbereit.
Man sollte wissen, dass es für Kinder auf diesem Platz keine speziellen Angebote gibt.

Wonder experience here, the guy who showed us around was lovely ! Bathroom was immaculate , electric hook up and water next to a concrete slab ! Lovely little gardens ! We was given a 10% discount card for Australia zoo ! Really reccomend this site !
Had such a good stay thanks to Fay's hospitality. She has the biggest heart, miss her already ❤️

Ripper spot near Australia zoo, with one of the most friendly operators i have met. Fay was welcoming and helpful, and the facilities were very well kept and clean. Will be staying again when driving past

Such a lovely quiet park. The amenities were so clean and tidy, one of the best we have ever seen. Peter, the host, a lovely guy and would help with anything. So close to Australia Zoo. A must to stay if u are in Beerwah!
Kerrie & Paul
Best bday ever  everyone sung happy bday to me and the owner nicest guy ever heard I thing and gave me a present in the morning definitely recommend this place 👍
Very clean, very welcoming. Would definitely recommend

This is an excellent no-frills independently owned caravan park. We would not hesitate to stay again and frankly wish we could have stayed longer although we visited fleetingly to visit Australia zoo (only 8 mins from this park!)

Fay is a wonderful host who met us at the gate and showed us to our site, great lady to have a chat with and she has excellent recommendations for local things to do. Nothing is too much bother and she even offered us an umbrella while we unhitched as it was pouring rain when we arrived.

The park has a high number of long-term residents, all of whom we encountered were very friendly and most have gone to effort to make their areas pretty with fairy lights, flower and herb gardens.

The sites are roomy and the ablutions were exceptionally clean and well maintained, probably the cleanest we have seen in a whole lap of Aus!!

Thank you Fay for having us!
Great place for stargazing. Fay was super awesome lady.. thank you so much Fay.

Good location to stay if travelling in motorhome or caravan if you are going to the Australia zoo, good pricing level and easy access to sites clean amenities and friendly staff... Thankyou to Fay for making us feel welcome...
It's clean and tidy, small space, a bit out of the way, full of residents. Alike other reviews, not the friendliest.. bit odd.

Camping gelegen  agrarisch gebied en stik bos waar veel vogels voorkomen. Rustige camping en ruime plekken voor de motorhome. Super nette en schone wc en douches. Zeer vriendelijke en behulpzame beheerster. Zeker een aanrader.

REALLY great caravan park! Just 5 mins out of Beerwah in the rural part, and close to Ustralia Zoo. The park was just SO NEAT, not squashed, large sites, and yhe amenities were so, so clean....never have I cone across a park yhat is so well kept, and maintained. The manager "Faye" was lovely, and nothing was too much trouble. Wrll done Faye! Both my wife and I loved staying in this very quiet park, and can thouroughly recommend it to anybody looking forward to enjoy a quality park and well cared for. Warren and Sue.

Love this Caravan Park, Fay n Peter are very lovely hosts.  Have stayed here a number of times, we needed to extend our stay due to an urgent matter and they were able to accommodate us. Thank you.  Cheers

really enjoyed our little stay! very cute and small caravan ! pete was very friendly and helpful
thank you
best caravan park we have stayed in . Ablution block the cleanest I have seen. Fay our host was a wealth of information and extremely helpful.

Best little caravan park I've been to yet. Sites are spacious. Pet friendly. Fay is an awesome host. There is a quaint area that provides free herbs. This camp is surrounded by strawberry 🍓 fields. Everyone is so friendly. The amenities are immaculate. It is a really quiet and tranquil spot. We will definitely be back.

What a wonderful place to stay. Fay was a pleasure to deal with and such a lovely lady. The caravan park has extremely clean amenities and you can tell the park is taken well care of.
A quiet place to spend time with your family and visit Australia Zoo in close proximity.
Thank you again and would definitely go back and stay there.

We have been here for a week now between the off grid camping and now in one of the King safari tents. As a young family I cannot rate this place better. Between the pool and water slides, dining and bistro and the truly incredible staff here. I don’t think you will find a better stay in the Sunshine Coast as an all included stay.

Staff are wonderful, friendly and very helpful. We stayed on the river and were lucky enough to have the place to ourselves - most of the time ;)
Mozzies are horrible but with plenty of bug spray, coils and long clothing, it’s ok. (Not ingenia’s fault though.
Kids loved the pool and the slides! And the pool side dinner made it great for parents to supervise and relax!
With great food options and awesome happy hour, even better when it’s extended!

Wir waren hier mit einem Campervan und es hat uns sehr gut gefallen. Es ist alles sauber. Es gibt Duschen mit Fön und ein tolles Café. Die Mitarbeiter sind sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit. Für Kinder wird auch einiges geboten und man kann jede Menge Spaß dort haben. Der Kleinkinder Pool ist jedoch etwas tief für die ganz Kleinen (50cm).

Great park to stay at, I stayed here with my young family of 5.
The weather was not ideal, there were plenty of activities when the rain went away.

We had a powered van site, the facilities are clean, new and the pool and slides are fantastic. All the staff are wonderful and they really do care for the kids having a good time, not just running kids activities, genuinely wanting all to have a great stay. Highly recommended. List of activities in the school holidays. We will be back.
Only gripe in the food at the restaurant isn't that great but overall the park experience is great! Great services and great staff. Just need to ban the kids on electric scooters.

Spent a week with family split over 4 premium powered sites with kids from 16 months to 6yrs old during the peak Christmas period. Everyone had different style setups/ vans but each site was extremely spacious (huge to be honest).  We were super lucky to score 4 sites together to enjoy our Family vacay.

Facilities were thoroughly cleaned and maintained daily including lawn care, fully equipped camp kitchen, coin/ tap & pay laundry blocks, dump point, numerous amenties blocks and a sensational pool area.  Plenty of entertainment options for kids with carts, putt putt, jumping castles etc.

The bar/bistro has a great menu variety, reasonably priced plus daily food vans visit to mix up options for guests. QR codes on your site and throughout the pool and bistro means drinks and food is quickly and easily delivered to you.  Pool was cleaned daily and both adults and kids enjoyed the slides.    Fishing from the river/ jetty enjoyed by all.

Dogs are permitted on ground floor of bar/ bistro area and all owners adhered to rules to keep dogs on leash and under control during our stay.

Location is close to local beaches, caltex, IGA, bottle shop, and not far from main town, shopping centre and water sports.

Daily updates texted to guests each day on specials or information.   Numerous thefts occurred during our stay which staff kept guests informed and on alert so do ensure you lock items of value away at night or when unattended.  Not parks fault just optimistic thieves ruining peoples holidays unfortunately.

Would stay again.
Toilets blocked bit of wasteland beside river not suitable for swimming has bull sharks in the river 100 perc better if u can get into cotton tree  PinCushion camp on sea and  mouth of  river
In the middle of paradise, but photos don’t seem like the real thing

Hello ,can not fault the service .you guys went out of your way for us . One think i would like to point out though is the magpie problem . I realize it is out of your hands however we could not eat outside the airstream caravan as ther was bird poo literally everywhere , i would recommend a daily clean on the mat and the table and chairs . Then we can clean whatever happens during the day . It definetly built up over the week we stayed and looked disgusting and we could not eat outside … thanks again .

Went here for Christmas with family, staff that were on were on were really helpful nothing was too much trouble and overall it was a very relaxing getaway. My niece even had a little injury in the park and the on site manager was there to help every step of the way.

Girls weekend away and couldn’t have asked for a better place to stay in our van. Large sites, food was awesome, loved the happy hour and cocktail selection. Lots for little and big kids. Definitely a gem of a place! Will be back with the family for sure
Good people ' Great service.👍

We loved it so much here we decided to stay longer!! Staff and facilities were fantastic, lawn sites are level and so lovely to park on. Super busy on weekends with locals using the restaurant/pool but mid week was a delight

This was our first stop on our northbound road trip. The kids had a great time here. We stayed in a family tent, which was the perfect size. The BBQ facilities were brand new, and the pool was the kids’ favourite!

Riverside is the gem I didn't know we were looking for. The caravan sites are very generous, the amenities are new, tidy, well done, and plentiful around the park. The pool is a great size for lots of people, and the slides are a winner for kids big and small. The on-site restaurant and bar are also awesome, with good portion sizes and reasonable prices. The only downside is that you can't bring your own drinks or food into the pool area. Otherwise, it's a pretty perfect spot.
Being next to the creek, mosquitoes are a thing to manage.

We stayed on a deluxe powered right across from the pool. Our site was perfect.
Pool was lovely, food, cocktails, and service was great. Our kids had a ball.
Stopped in for 1 night on my travels. Ran into owner at the bar/restaurant. Had a few beers and a steak. Had a fantastic stay and can't wait to bring the family back sometime
Staff are so nice, welcoming and made sure we are well looked after!
One of the best places I been with my family

We are usually very much resort people and so booked this glamping tent not really knowing what to expect and we were very impressed! |The tent was clean and spacious, the towels and linen were hotel quality and the bed was super comfortable. |The park facilities were outstanding, a great pool and bar area and the grounds are beautifully maintained.|The tent felt very private and had a lovely lawn area and view of the river.|The staff were very welcoming and friendly, from check in to check out.|Highly recommend this spot and we will be back again next time we visit the Sunshine Coast.

We absolutely loved our stay at ingenia holidays at rivershore! We stayed in our caravan so can’t comment on rooms, but there are heaps of kid friendly activities including a wonderful pool with awesome slides, a park, go-karts, putt putt golf and due to it being Halloween when we stayed they had trick or treating, a scavenger hunt, discos and movie nights (even though it wasn’t in school holidays)!

The staff are incredibly friendly and willing to help at all points of your stay and the amenities are clean and up to date. When we shifted things around for our stay the staff were super willing to help. The park is very safe and secure as well - we went to K’gari for a week and came back to our caravan safe and sound 👌

The restaurant is excellent and staff were super accomodating of me being gluten intolerant, and there were already lots of gluten free options too. The caravan park is also only 15 mins from a few plaza with heaps of stores, so easily accessible for that, and lots of disability friendly ramps and spaces too.

We will absolutely be back! Thanks Ingenia Holidays!

I like t there the food and the slide was fun it was fun maybe I I should come back again next week it was fun I like it there it was fun me and Owen are where on the water slides it was fun maybe I should come. Back again it was fun I like like it it was fun it was fun

Newly renovated bathrooms were in disrepair and un functional. There was no hot water on 3/5 showers.
The main road is very noisy, there should not be campsites valued at over $100 per night positioned against it facing dumpsters and the dump point.
Most of the staff were very friendly except for one woman who was very put out when asked about peddle cart hire.
Would never stay here again and wouldn’t recommend
We had an enjoyable stay at Rivershore we would love to stay again
This is a must do the glamping Safari tents were amazing! So much to do!  the pool is fabulous

Amazing holiday park!
So much to do with the kids.
The heated  pool is awesome!

We stayed for 2 nights at the King Safari tent as a family of 4, and we were really happy with how clean, cozy, and beautiful it was. The facilities are excellent, and the general bathrooms are always kept clean. A staff member at the pool kept an eye on the children so they wouldn't run and be prone to accidents. The restaurant's food is outstanding, and there's something new every day. The restaurant staff are friendly and kind. The receptionists are super friendly and helpful. They had many extra activities for the children every day. They have an SMS system that keeps us informed about what is happening throughout the day, including activities, food specials, live music, weather, etc. My kids enjoyed it so much. We wish we had done more, as they have so many more things to do, like fishing and going to a night fire pit, but we needed more time. We had just stayed six nights in a villa at Oaks Resort in Caloundra before staying at Ingenia Rivershore, and the Ingenia Rivershore was ten times better. We wish we had stayed more days at Rivershore. We will come back.

Fantastic little caravan park. The amenities are spotless and the staff and permanent residents are lovely people.
The park is in a beautiful quiet spot near town with Australia Zoo just down the road as well as the beautiful Glass House mountains. Highly recommend it. Catered for our 31ft motorhome easily. Having travelled from WA we have stayed at a lot of parks and this one rates in our top 3 for sure.😊

Quiet, Clean and friendly sums up the experience of this independently owned caravan park.

The park manager (Peter) is friendly and his big but super chill dog buddy welcomes all guests.

Most are permanent residents, so the caravan park has quite the character of different setups and artistic deco, friendly residents and I love the herb garden that they allow us to pick from - which I did for the rosemary to add on to my cooking.
Very welcoming and friendly park. Thanks Faye for your hospitality. Everything super clean and user friendly. Will be back. Cheers and enjoy your cruise Mal and Kerry👍🤗

This is a quiet, tranquil caravan park amongst strawberry fields, only a short drive from town. They have established a beautiful and unique garden with pavilion for guests to enjoy. You can help yourself to fresh herbs if you like.
The team at Beerwah Caravan Park is very friendly and tirelessly working on the cleanliness and upkeep of the facilities. The amenities are absolutely spotless. They have truly been outstanding on our travelling journey.
We can highly recommend this caravan park to the rest of the RV community.

L’accueil était sympathique et chaleureux, les propriétaires sont très agréables et l’ambiance est conviviale. Nous avons apprécié notre nuit au milieu des champs de fraises !

Mention spéciale pour la propreté du site qui est fabuleuse !

Merci !
Vivi et Liza

Les propriétaires sont très serviables

Lovely place, an independent business tucked away in the middle of a working fruit farm. Nice people, excellent and clean facilities (showers, toilets, disabled ablution section too), and reasonably priced. A nice mix of long stayers and those passing through.

No website, you'll have to go old school and phone. The centre of the park are the showers and laundry, with a cooking or food prep area with microwaves, toaster oven, barbecue, hotplates and fridges. There are a range of condiments out for you to use, and it's all sparkling clean.

All these facilities are under cover, essential in Queensland, rain or shine. Lovely gardens you're welcome to pick herbs or what's in season from, and handing over your returnable bottles and cans to fund the garden is a nice way to give back.

There's a chemical toilet dump site (not under cover but flat access) and big bins for rubbish at that end of park too.

The working farm you drive through to get there is strawberries, lychees, pineapples and market veggies, but so far I haven't seen any sign of pick your own.

There is a fabulous-looking butcher down the road 100m, recommended by everyone I talk to), who does retail (and wholesale) sales. Not for the sensitive, as the cattle in the paddocks are the ones that end up in the butchery. They look happy and fat, no feed lots, just rich pastures.

You do see people picking the fruit, which must be a really hard job. Even when we visited in May (last month of Autumn), the sun was blazing and even locals were getting sunstroke.

The powered campsites are 10m X 13m, with a solid concrete pad on each one, lovely to have next to your van or tent for awnings and marquees. Water on tap too.

The quirky and friendly town of Beerwah (mix of tree changers, hippies, and  farming community locals), is maybe 5 minutes down the road, with a big Woolworths, an ALDI, and most things you need, including an excellent laundromat. There's also the train, if for some misguided reason you want to go to Brisbane, it's about an hour away.

Only about 25 mins to nearest Bunnings, there are several (on coast, or down Beerwah Forest road, or down M1 to Big Fish, or down to Morayfield). About 10 mins driving slowly to the Mitre10 at Glass House Mountains township but there's also a big hardware store in Beerwah, on right as you come into town along Simpson St, past the ALDI, and just past the turn for the Woolies, tucked behind the servo (petrol station) on right.

In Beerwah there's good free internet near or in the library, and in most places in town including the Beerwah Laundromat. At park it's sometimes okay, sometimes not, but better than our last stop in Glass House Mountains. Phone signal is excellent at the park for actual texts or calls.

All in all, a recommended stop, I've never felt so genuinely welcome. Thanks to Fay and Pete, and everyone else.

This caravan park was ideally situated in the Glass Mountains area. 15 minutes to Maleny, 25 minutes to Montville. We used it as a base to explore. But be warned as soon as the sun starts to go down the flying beetles are out and the cane toads! Not much you can do . Unfortunately I am horrified by the number of  toads so went into my van by 6.15pm every night.

Convenient  close to Landsborough ... views occluded .. ground small tidy surrounded by high trees. Facilites clean, basic and old. Very expensive for basic camp ground. Road noise.. quietens at night.

Don’t camp here. No unpowered sites, so we had to pay $50/day for two nights for water/power we didn’t use. Unfortunately, it wasn’t worth it. It was by the busy road and the entrance. The amnesties was 100 m down a dark trip hazard road to only one dud cold shower (don’t expect it to be available either!). Not nice during winter. There is a fabulous new indoor camp kitchen which was LOCKED. There is an outdoor basement one for plebs. It wouldn’t have hurt so much if we didn’t know there was a nice clean empty one above us. They put us next to the only three other caravans there too. Talk about privacy. No WIFI and mobile reception is patchy. Called “Ocean View” but it’s 20 km from it. More of a tiny hint of ocean if you squint from our spot. Our $50 was better spent in Narooma or one of the other beachside parks where we actually had an ‘Ocean View’. We just needed a place to stay quickly to see Australia Zoo. That’s the only plus about it besides nice grass and a lovely receptionist (closes at 1pm). We should known better. Just didn’t think it would be that bad.
The daily communication regarding upcoming activities and events was amazing. Staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating. Perfect entertainment for the kids!

We stayed for a week in the Glamping Tent Cabins, which were really luxurious.  There was a group of us and we had a private wedding on the grounds in the middle of the week.  We pre-ordered food platters from the Driftwood restaurant that is onsite, which were amazing. The customer service of the staff is definitely 5 star, they are always helpful and seem very happy. If you don't mind a 10pm quiet curfew this is the perfect place to hold a destination wedding.
Thank you Ingenia - we had the perfect week.

I would highly recommend hiring the King Safari tent they are beautiful,calming and relaxing.
Staff very friendly an helpful

Even though it wasn’t holidays there were plenty of kids, not really my favourite people but even the camp sites on the creek further away from the noise were almost the same price as the one we had. The Park certainly don’t miss opportunities to value add, almost every activity had a charge.

Very nice place to stay,play,fish and food truck for dinner so much to do. Highly recommend. Caravan spots are great size amenities are all great and clean. Toilets nice and clean females toilets have hair-dryers. Laundry has coins and also has card so perfect if you don't have the coins. So much space for thr kids to run around and nice area for fishing and also ramp for boat or jetski. This place has it all. Fire pit available so the little ones can put there marshmallows on and have a great night with others.

Wonderful holiday resort. The staff is absolutely amazing and so friendly. There are so many activities for the whole family. The waterside and pool area was so much fun and a very generous size. Loved the cabanas for relaxing and the pool side service. The food is incredible. I loved the salmon eggs benedict. We had several dishes throughout our stay, and every dish from the pizza and wings to the eggs benadict was delicious. We stayed in a glammping tent, and it was amazing (more like a home away from home) it took camping to a whole new level. Nice deep soaker tub and shower in the bathroom. They have a  restraunt on site that you can order food straight to your site or glamping tent for delivery or to the poolside or dine in. They also make some nice cocktails. The amenities included a pool and waterslides, jumping pillow, pedal trikes, mini golf, laundry, showers, 2 large bonfire pits, kids' movie nights, scavenger hunts, colouring contests, and more.

We recently stayed here with our van and had the ultimate spot right in front of the pool. The amenities are clean and the grounds are kept in immaculate condition. For those with vans the sites are quite large compared to other parks in the area and the staff are more than happy to assist. The park has plenty of room away from the vans and sites to kick a ball or ride a bike or even cast a line in.

We ate at the restaurant a few times and the food was delicious and reasonably priced. Each night had a theme and on the weekends there was even bingo and other games.

A great park and one which we have already booked to return to again.

Fabulous weekend here camping with kids. Kids loved the great pool, slides, water play pool, go-carts, mini golf and huge jumping mat. Adults loved the great service, spotless facilities, camp kitchens, cafe, bar and live music on weekend afternoons. Would definitely recommend!

We had a fantastic family holiday at Ingenia Rivershore. The staff were a pleasure to talk to at every stage of our stay, making us feel really at home.
